summary.en: Most companies bundle three fundamentally different businesses inside one entity: customer relationships, product innovation, and infrastructure. Each has its own economics and culture. Unbundling means separating them into independent firms or operating units to remove the conflicts that inevitably arise.
summary.zh: 大多数公司把三种本质不同的业务捆绑在一家实体里:客户关系、产品创新、基础设施。它们各有自己的经济、竞争和文化驱动力。解绑就是把它们拆分到独立的公司或事业部,消除捆绑必然带来的内生冲突。

sources.label: John Hagel III & Marc Singer · 'Unbundling the Corporation' · Harvard Business Review · March 1999
Michael Treacy & Fred Wiersema · The Discipline of Market Leaders · Addison-Wesley · 1995
Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley · 2010 (Pattern No. 1, pp. 56-65)

references.label: John Hagel III & Marc Singer · 'Unbundling the Corporation' · Harvard Business Review

note.en: Originating paper. Names the three business types (customer relationship, product innovation, infrastructure) and argues bundling forces unavoidable strategic compromises.
note.zh: 原始论文。提出三类业务(客户关系、产品创新、基础设施)的概念,论证把它们捆绑在一家公司里必然导致战略妥协。

references.label: Michael Treacy & Fred Wiersema · The Discipline of Market Leaders · Addison-Wesley

note.en: Pre-Hagel framing of three value disciplines (customer intimacy, product leadership, operational excellence) that map almost 1:1 onto the three unbundled business types.
note.zh: Hagel 之前的奠基性框架。提出三种价值规则(客户亲密、产品领先、运营卓越),与解绑后的三类业务几乎一一对应。

references.label: Osterwalder & Pigneur · Business Model Generation · Wiley

note.en: Adapts unbundling into the BMC pattern catalog (Pattern No. 1). Uses Swiss private banking and mobile telecom as the two paradigm cases — both shipped in this library.
note.zh: 把解绑收录为 BMC 模式手册(第 1 号模式)。书中两个范式案例:瑞士私人银行业、移动电信业 —— 本案例库均已收录。
